title: asperula serotina
intro: Asperula serotina, also known as Wood Sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and small, white or pink flowers. The plant is rich in ascorbic acid, or vitamin C, which is responsible for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Asperula serotina has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, and its health benefits include improving iron absorption and boosting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and is often used in herbal remedies to support overall health and well-being.
